So BP decides to turn the Gulf of Mexico into a chocolate milkshake, because apparently 11 lives and basic environmental responsibility are just suggestions when there's oil to be drilled.

Suddenly every fisherman in the Gulf is staring at water that looks like my morning coffee, except this coffee killed everything it touched and made their livelihoods disappear faster than my will to live.
Now shrimp costs more than gold because apparently when you poison an entire ecosystem, the remaining seafood becomes as rare as honest politicians.
West Coast fishermen are working overtime like they're the only sober drivers at closing time, because everyone suddenly remembered the Pacific Ocean exists.
California avocado farmers see dollar signs bigger than their water bills and start planting these green gold nuggets like they're preparing for the apocalypse.
Plot twist: turns out you can't just magically grow infinite avocados, and now guacamole costs more than my rent because supply and demand is apparently a foreign concept.

And here we are, two years later, where some poor taco shop owner named Mike has to charge EIGHTEEN DOLLARS for a burrito because of AVOCADOS, and honestly I'm about to lose my absolute mind over the fact that a BURRITO costs more than a decent bottle of wine, what kind of dystopian nightmare timeline are we living in where MEXICAN FOOD is a luxury item?!
